threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus like it's reliable. Locals know it’s more a suggestion than a schedule.
Sat on the 37 last night, a woman in a pink feather boa serenaded a group of bemused tourists. Classic Brixton vibes—always a show on the night bus.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a street vendor selling vegan döner at 3 AM while a group of artists painted a mural nearby. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you catch a bus that smells like legit currywurst at 3 AM after the club. It's oddly comforting amidst the chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they can name the pop-up taco truck that’s been parked outside The Abbey for the last three months.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy on the bus passionately explaining the history of the Tenderloin to a confused tourist. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss how locals use the quiet back alleys for shortcuts. They stick to the main streets and miss half the charm of Gràcia.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their confused look when they try to use the metro ticket machines; locals just glide through while they fumble.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube.' Locals know it's the Overground when you're in Hackney. It's that small badge of local pride.
Caught the 59 last night; an old chap lectured a teenager on the history of reggae. Everyone was glued to their phones, but he had a captive audience by the last stop.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids turning an abandoned car into a mini skate park. Only in Neukölln do the things left behind become a playground.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a techno party that starts in a skate park and ends at sunrise by the Spree. It's the rhythm of the city.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they actually use the bus schedule and don’t just complain about delays – real locals adapt to the chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy doing yoga on the back of a crowded Muni at Civic Center. The tourists were all filming—it’s one way to cope with the chaos!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the parks here are just for picnics. But locals know they’re the stage for spontaneous concerts and hidden art shows.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones carrying guidebooks and stopping to take pictures of every building, even the plain ones. Locals just walk by.
